title: solanum tuberosum subsp. andigenum
intro: Solanum tuberosum subsp. andigenum, also known as the Andean potato, is a subspecies of the Solanum tuberosum plant, specifically native to the Andean region of South America. The tubers of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The Andean potato offers several health benefits, including lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and aiding in the management of diabetes. The tubers can be prepared as herbal tea, dried powder, or raw in various dishes, allowing for diverse applications in herbal medicine.
